content_hints.description: The Trading Dock is built into the shell of a tai-na -- a living island, its carapace encrusted with barnacles the size of boulders and vegetation that has taken root in crem deposits centuries deep. The dock bobs gently as the greatshell breathes. The sea stretches in every direction, turquoise and violent, dotted with the dark shapes of other island-creatures drifting on the current.
content_hints.read_aloud: You step onto a dock built on the back of a god. The tai-na's carapace stretches in every direction, encrusted with centuries of barnacles and vegetation rooted in crem deposits deep enough to farm. The dock bobs gently as the creature breathes -- each inhalation a tidal shift that rocks boats against their moorings. The sea beyond is turquoise and impossible, dotted with the dark shapes of other island-creatures drifting on currents that obey no wind. The dockmaster approaches, walking with the rolling gait of someone who has lived their entire life on a surface that breathes.

npcs.name: Dockmaster Taravangian
npcs.dialogue: Welcome to the Reshi Isles. The island you stand on is called Akak'ar by the locals -- it means 'the patient one.' The tai-na has not moved in three years. When it does, the dock goes with it. Plan accordingly.
npcs.notes: This Taravangian is a Reshi islander who shares a name with the king of Kharbranth. He is oblivious to the coincidence. He knows the tai-na's behavior intimately and has noticed it becoming agitated.

npcs.name: Sailor Kaza
npcs.dialogue: Passage between islands costs ten chips. Passage during a highstorm costs your life. I do not offer refunds on either.
npcs.notes: Kaza is a Soulcaster who has used the device so often that her body is beginning to transform into smoke at the edges. She hides it beneath loose clothing. She knows the deep-water routes between islands.

content_hints.investigation_success: Kaza's hands shimmer at the edges -- not a trick of light, but a transformation. She is partially Soulcast, her flesh transitioning to smoke. She has perhaps months before the process becomes irreversible. She does not seem frightened. She seems eager.
content_hints.perception_success: The tai-na shifts -- a grinding of shell against coral reef -- and the entire dock tilts five degrees. The locals do not even look up. But beneath the physical shift, you feel something else: a vibration identical to the resonance from the Stone Shambles. The tai-na is responding to the same source.
event_triggers: On entry: The tai-na shifts -- a grinding of shell against coral reef -- and the entire dock tilts five degrees. The locals do not even look up. But the tai-na's eye, visible through a gap in the carapace below the dock, rotates to track your arrival. It has not done that for any other visitor.

content_hints.description: The Shell Market occupies a natural bowl in the tai-na's carapace where merchants spread their wares on living shell that grows beneath their stalls. Fish still gleaming with salt water, pearls harvested from lesser greatshells, spices from the mainland, and curiosities dredged from the sea floor -- including pieces of ancient technology no one can identify.
content_hints.read_aloud: Commerce thrives on the back of a living island. Merchants spread their wares on shell that grows beneath their stalls -- literally: the tai-na's carapace regenerates so quickly that permanent structures are impossible. Fish gleam with salt water. Pearls glow with inner light. And at one stall, curiosities dredged from forty fathoms: pieces of worked stone and metal that hum when touched, warm to the skin, bearing glyphs that match the inscriptions in the Stone Shambles. The deep water holds secrets older than the islands.

npcs.name: Pearl Diver Gu
npcs.dialogue: These pearls come from the belly of a santhid -- killed my net, broke my boat, nearly broke me. But a santhid pearl is worth more than a gemheart to the right buyer. You look like the right buyer.
npcs.notes: Gu has dived deeper than any Reshi and seen structures on the ocean floor -- stone buildings beneath the waves, their architecture identical to the Stone Shambles. He does not understand what he saw. He drew it on fish-leather and shows no one.

npcs.name: Curiosity Seller Axies
npcs.dialogue: I collect oddities. This piece I found at forty fathoms -- it is warm to the touch and hums when a highstorm approaches. Fabrial? Maybe. But it predates fabrial science by several epochs. Interesting, yes?
npcs.notes: Axies is an Aimian -- a race older than humanity on Roshar. He collects artifacts from the deep water because he recognizes them. He will not say from where. His tattoos shift when he lies.

content_hints.investigation_success: The deep-sea artifact bears the same glyph as the aluminum key from the Stone Shambles vault. It is a fragment of a larger mechanism -- a second key, or a lock component. The two pieces vibrate in sympathetic resonance when brought within arm's reach.
content_hints.perception_success: A santhid surfaces near the dock -- the first seen in months. Its enormous eye, visible above the waterline, tracks not the divers scrambling for gear, but you specifically. Axies drops everything and starts sketching, his tattoos rearranging themselves into patterns of excitement.
event_triggers: On entry: A santhid surfaces near the dock -- the first seen in months. Divers scramble for their gear. Axies drops everything and starts sketching. The santhid's eye, the size of a wagon wheel, fixes on you with an intelligence that predates the Oathpact.

content_hints.description: The Island Spine Trail follows the tai-na's dorsal ridge upward -- a path carved into living shell by generations of Reshi climbers. Handholds are cut at regular intervals, their edges worn smooth by ten thousand grips. The wind intensifies with altitude, carrying salt spray and the distant singing of Reshi navigators using tonal markers to track the island's drift.
content_hints.read_aloud: The path follows the tai-na's spine -- a dorsal ridge of living shell that rises toward the creature's highest point. Handholds carved by generations of climbers are worn smooth as glass. The wind intensifies with every step, carrying salt spray and something else: the singing of Reshi navigators, their tonal markers tracking the island's drift in harmonics that sound, to your ears attuned by the Stone Shambles, remarkably like the rhythms of the Dawnsingers. Lichen colonies cover the shell in patterns that are not random.

npcs.name: Reshi Climber Nym
npcs.dialogue: The tai-na knows we are here. It tolerates us because we clean the parasites from its shell. Fair trade, yes? You want to reach the summit, climb steady and do not anger the lichen colonies. They bite.
npcs.notes: Nym is the best climber on the island. She has reached the tai-na's eye cavity -- a journey no one else has survived. She found a door inside the creature. A door made of stone, set into living chitin. She has told no one.

enemies.name: Parasitic Cremling Colony
enemies.notes: Colony creature. Lives in tai-na's shell. Attacks perceived threats to host.
special: Symbiotic Alert: If colony takes damage, tai-na shifts, DC 12 or all targets knocked prone

content_hints.investigation_success: The lichen patterns on the shell spell words in Dawnsinger script -- the tai-na's carapace is a living document, its surface inscribed by organisms that write in a language older than humanity. The message, translated: 'I remember the oath. I keep the door.'
content_hints.perception_success: The shell beneath your feet is warm -- the tai-na's body heat radiating through its carapace. But the warmth is not uniform. It concentrates in channels that follow the same patterns as the Stormlight conduits in the Stone Shambles. The tai-na is not merely a creature. It is a living fabrial.
event_triggers: On entry: The shell beneath your feet is warm -- the tai-na's body heat radiating through its carapace. You are climbing a living thing. Melishi presses her hand to the shell and gasps: 'It is aware. Not just alive -- aware. It knows why we are here.'

content_hints.description: The Greatshell Summit crowns the tai-na's highest point -- a plateau of ancient shell where the Reshi king holds court. The king sits cross-legged on a throne of polished coral, attended by advisors who communicate in a mixture of spoken language and hand signs readable from a hundred feet away. The view encompasses three other island-creatures and the open sea beyond.
content_hints.read_aloud: The summit is a throne room without walls, open to sky and sea on every side. The Reshi king sits cross-legged on polished coral, attended by advisors whose hand-signs carry across the wind as clearly as shouts. Three other tai-na are visible from here -- dark shapes on the horizon, each carrying its own nation on its back. The king studies you with eyes that hold the weight of governance without borders. Behind him, the sky darkens. Not with clouds. With something that presses against the boundary between what you can see and what exists on the other side.

npcs.name: King Taravangian of the Reshi (island king)
npcs.dialogue: I am king of a nation that moves. My borders are whatever the tai-na can see. My army is the sea itself. What I lack is knowledge -- knowledge of what stirs in the deep water. Something has driven the santhids to the surface. Something has made the tai-na restless. I need someone brave or foolish to find out what.
npcs.notes: The island king is genuinely frightened. The tai-na has been humming a subsonic note for three weeks. The other tai-na have changed course to converge on this position. Something in the deep water is calling them.

npcs.name: Royal Navigator Rysn
npcs.dialogue: The tai-na has changed course three times this month. It has never done that before. The currents have not shifted. Something in the deep water is pulling it -- or pushing it away.
npcs.notes: Rysn can communicate with the tai-na through a bond she does not fully understand. She senses its emotions: fear, determination, and something she can only describe as 'remembering.' The tai-na is remembering an obligation older than human civilization.

content_hints.investigation_success: Rysn's navigation charts show the convergence pattern of all nearby tai-na. They are not drifting randomly. They are forming a circle around a point in the deep water. That point corresponds to the location described in the Guardian Construct's final message: the place where the boundary between realms is thinnest.
content_hints.perception_success: The king's throne vibrates. The tai-na is humming -- a subsonic vibration that the Reshi have never heard before. The navigators look frightened. But Melishi looks exalted. She recognizes the frequency. It is the Stoneward resonance. The tai-na is singing the same note as the ruins.
event_triggers: On entry: The king's throne vibrates. The tai-na is humming -- a subsonic vibration that the Reshi have never heard before. The navigators look frightened. The sky behind the king darkens with something that is not cloud. The boundary between realms shimmers.

content_hints.description: The Tai-na's Eye. A natural cavity in the greatshell's carapace, directly above one of the creature's massive eyes -- a disk of dark chitin twenty feet across, its surface iridescent with bioluminescence. Looking through the translucent shell into the eye beneath, you see something that should not be there: a chamber of worked stone inside the living creature, visible through layers of chitin like a fossil in amber.
content_hints.read_aloud: The cavity opens above the tai-na's eye -- twenty feet of dark chitin, translucent as stained glass, its surface crawling with bioluminescence. Beneath: the eye itself, a disk of impossible intelligence that tracks your shadow through the shell. And deeper, visible through layers of living chitin like a fossil viewed through amber: a chamber of worked stone. Inside the creature. Not swallowed. Built. As if the tai-na grew around a building that was here first, incorporating architecture into anatomy the way coral incorporates stone.

enemies.name: Greatshell Parasite (Symbiont)
enemies.notes: Lives in the eye cavity. Protects the chamber door. Part crustacean, part spren-touched.
special: Shell Armor: Reduces first 5 damage per hit
Bioluminescent Flash: DC 12 or blinded for 1 round

loot.name: Ancient Stone Fragment (from inside the tai-na)

content_hints.investigation_success: The stone chamber inside the tai-na contains a door -- identical in design to the Oathgates of Urithiru. The tai-na did not swallow a building. A building was planted in it, the way the Stone Shamans plant trees for the dead. The tai-na is a living Oathgate, and the door inside it opens to the Cognitive Realm.
content_hints.perception_success: The tai-na blinks. The eye beneath you closes and opens, and for a moment you see the stone chamber clearly -- it contains a door. A door with an aluminum keyhole. The same shape as the key from the Stone Shambles vault. The tai-na shifts, pressing the eye closer to the surface, as if showing you something it wants you to see.
event_triggers: On entry: The tai-na blinks. The eye beneath you closes and opens, and for a moment you see the stone chamber inside the creature clearly -- it contains a door. A door that is opening from the other side.

content_hints.description: Deep Waters. You descend from the island's edge into the sea -- warm, impossibly clear, lit by bioluminescent organisms that trail like constellations in the current. Below, a corrupted greatshell circles the tai-na's base, its carapace veined with dark violet light that pulses in the rhythm of the Everstorm. Voidspren swarm around it like pilot fish around a shark. It has been feeding on the tai-na. It has been growing.
content_hints.read_aloud: The sea receives you in warmth and impossible clarity. Bioluminescent organisms trail through the water like constellations given liquid form. Below, circling the tai-na's base with the patience of a predator that has already won: a greatshell veined with Voidlight, its carapace pulsing violet-black in the rhythm of the Everstorm. Voidspren swarm around it in spiraling clouds, feeding on the boundary between realms that the tai-na guards. The corrupted greatshell turns toward you. Its eyes glow violet instead of natural blue. Its mandibles open. From its throat comes a word spoken in a language that predates the Oathpact, a word that resonates with the Cognitive Realm boundary itself: 'ODIUM.'

enemies.name: Corrupted Greatshell (Void-Touched)
enemies.notes: Corrupted by Odium's Investiture. Voidlight-veined carapace. Attacks the tai-na if not engaged. Destroying it seals the Cognitive breach.
special: Voidlight Pulse: 20ft sphere, 2d8 spirit damage, DC 14 or frightened
Corruption Aura: All Stormlight within 15ft drains 1 point per round

enemies.name: Voidspren Swarm
enemies.notes: Swarm. Half damage from single attacks. Feeds on Stormlight.
special: Investiture Drain: Steals 1d4 Focus/Stormlight on hit

content_hints.investigation_success: The corrupted greatshell was not merely touched by Voidlight. It was sent. A deliberate probe from the Cognitive Realm, testing the boundary the tai-na guards. Destroying it seals the breach temporarily. Using the aluminum key from the Stone Shambles in the tai-na's internal door seals it permanently -- but requires someone to turn the key from the Cognitive side. Someone would have to stay.
content_hints.perception_success: The corrupted greatshell turns toward you with eyes that glow violet instead of natural blue. It opens mandibles wide enough to swallow a boat. But it is not looking at you. It is looking at the aluminum key, if you carry it. It recognizes it. It fears it. The key is the one thing that can close the door it came through.
event_triggers: On entry: The corrupted greatshell turns toward you with eyes that glow violet instead of natural blue. It opens mandibles wide enough to swallow a boat, and from its throat comes a sound that is not a roar -- it is a word, spoken in a language that predates the Oathpact. The word is 'ODIUM.' The tai-na shudders beneath you. The boundary between realms tears open.
